rationale.summary: Vote: NO. For this InfoAction (expired), downside risk and/or weak safeguards outweighed demonstrated benefit in the current evidence set. Decisive reason: a material claim is unsupported — "The majority of the funds in this budget will be disbursed to DeFi protocols including decentralized exchanges (DEXs) and lending protocols to help seed liquidity on Cardano." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "50,000,000 ADA is requested over the course of 12 months (the duration of the budget) to support the Cardano decentralized finance (DeFi) ecosystem." and clears the evidence gates. What would change this vote to YES: Use stable, reproducible anchor links (commit-pinned when possible); Clarify governance intent and expected measurable outcome. Residual uncertainty: DRep ratification support is below threshold; this is not treated as active opposition. Confidence context: score -0.32; confidence 60.1%.
rationale.summary_raw: Doctrine-aware reasoning layer nudged the score by -0.040 (clamped to ±0.05): Doctrine-aware offline lean for a info action: 1 well-supported claim(s), 6 unsupported high-materiality claim(s). Net bounded adjustment -0.040 (clamped to ±0.05).
top_fixes: Use stable, reproducible anchor links (commit-pinned when possible).
Clarify governance intent and expected measurable outcome.
Map each major claim to primary receipts and stable source links.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Cardano Treasury DeFi Liquidity Budget
Stated amount: not stated in document

Deliverables: The goal of this proposal is to increase liquidity on Cardano, specifically by supporting stablecoin liquidity., 50,000,000 ADA is requested over the course of 12 months (the duration of the budget) to support the Cardano decentralized finance (DeFi) ecosystem., The majority of the funds in this budget will be disbursed to DeFi protocols including decentralized exchanges (DEXs) and lending protocols to help seed liquidity on Cardano., The funds are managed by a 7 person administrating committee composed of publicly identified individuals who are part of multi-signature (multisig) wallet from which liquidity will be managed.
Claim (other, proposer_asserted, low materiality): The goal of this proposal is to increase liquidity on Cardano, specifically by supporting stablecoin liquidity.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): 50,000,000 ADA is requested over the course of 12 months (the duration of the budget) to support the Cardano decentralized finance (DeFi) ecosystem.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The majority of the funds in this budget will be disbursed to DeFi protocols including decentralized exchanges (DEXs) and lending protocols to help seed liquidity on Cardano.

Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The funds are managed by a 7 person administrating committee composed of publicly identified individuals who are part of multi-signature (multisig) wallet from which liquidity will be managed.

Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): High Level Overview Amount - The Cardano treasury should dedicate 3.33% of treasury funds (50 million ada) to purchasing fiat backed stablecoins, spread out over 12 monthly purchases.

Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The committee should make every effort to help mitigate the potential price impact of converting ADA to fiat to mint as stable coins over the 12 month period.
missing: Independent evidence for: The majority of the funds in this budget will be disbursed to DeFi protocols including decentralized exchanges (DEXs) and lending protocols to help seed liquidity on Cardano.

Independent evidence for: The funds are managed by a 7 person administrating committee composed of publicly identified individuals who are part of multi-signature (multisig) wallet from which liquidity will be managed.

Independent evidence for: High Level Overview Amount - The Cardano treasury should dedicate 3.33% of treasury funds (50 million ada) to purchasing fiat backed stablecoins, spread out over 12 monthly purchases.
Independent evidence for: The committee should make every effort to help mitigate the potential price impact of converting ADA to fiat to mint as stable coins over the 12 month period.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "50,000,000 ADA is requested over the course of 12 months (the duration of the budget) to support the Cardano decentralized finance (DeFi) ecosystem." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The majority of the funds in this budget will be disbursed to DeFi protocols including decentralized exchanges (DEXs) and lending protocols to help seed liquidity on Cardano." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
